hypothesis.statement: Electronic modification of Cu surface states by neighboring Au atoms is the primary driver of C2H4 selectivity in Cu-Au electrodes, not CO spillover. The Au d-band interaction shifts the Cu d-band center, weakening H* binding and suppressing HER while maintaining CO* binding for C-C coupling.

scope.conditions_summary: Any Cu-Au configuration including alloys, interfaces, and patterned electrodes

generalizable_to.basis: Ag has a different d-band position than Au, so the electronic effect may differ significantly

source_details.query: Compare adsorption energies on Cu(111), Au(111), Cu3Au(111)

reasoning_chain.statement: UMA shows Cu3Au(111) has dramatically different H* binding (E_ads = +0.16 eV) compared to Cu(111) (E_ads = -0.07 eV), indicating Au strongly modifies Cu electronic structure
Weaker H* binding on Cu3Au suppresses HER, redirecting selectivity toward CO2RR products
CO binding on Cu3Au(111) is +0.04 eV (vs -0.42 eV on Cu(111)), suggesting the electronic effect may be too strong for C-C coupling — intermediate compositions may be optimal
If electronic modification dominates, C2H4 selectivity should correlate with Au content in the surface layer, not with geometric patterning

mechanism.description: Au atoms in or near the Cu surface modify the Cu d-band center via ligand and strain effects, weakening H* binding (suppressing HER) while shifting CO* binding to an intermediate strength favorable for C-C coupling.
mechanism.rate_limiting_step: C-C coupling (CO* + CO* -> OCCO*) on electronically modified Cu sites

predictions.magnitude: HER suppressed by >50% on Cu-Au vs pure Cu
predictions.falsification_criterion: If HER is unchanged on Cu-Au alloy surfaces vs pure Cu, electronic modification of H* binding is not significant

predictions.magnitude: C2H4 FE should correlate with surface Au fraction, not domain spacing
predictions.falsification_criterion: If two electrodes with the same Au surface fraction but different domain spacings show different C2H4 FE, geometry (not electronics) matters

predictions.magnitude: d-band center shift of 0.2-0.5 eV for Cu sites adjacent to Au
predictions.falsification_criterion: If DFT shows <0.1 eV d-band shift for Cu adjacent to Au, electronic modification is negligible

confidence.basis: UMA shows large binding energy changes on Cu3Au, but the geometry-dependent experimental data (80um vs 160um stripe width matters) is hard to explain by pure electronic effects since both geometries have the same Au surface fraction.

descriptors.name: hypothesis_status
descriptors.definition: Current hypothesis status after evidence evaluation.

descriptors.name: hypothesis_score
descriptors.definition: Net evidence score: +1 per supporting, -1 per contradicting.

links.notes: Alternative mechanism to CO spillover hypothesis. Both explain C2H4 enhancement but make different predictions about geometry dependence vs composition dependence.
